title: Business Breakfast | Stock/Share Market News | 29th April 2026 || TV5 Money Live

recommendations.note: Put the modest amount into one name rather than four diversified
recommendations.detail: Asked by a caller with Rs 50,000 and a 3-year horizon to pick one of four (L&T, Infosys, Vikram Solar, MCX), Kutumba Rao said the amount is very modest and he would rather put it all into a single name, L&T, than spread it across four diversified stocks.

recommendations.note: Wait for results; range-bound, very high PE
recommendations.detail: Results had not yet come; he expects no great surprises and a range-bound stock between 180 and 230. With a very high PE (~800+ pre-consolidation), he said this set of results will be key and could dictate the future, so wait for the results.

recommendations.note: At bottom valuation; good for one-year-plus horizon
recommendations.detail: Asked alongside Jio Finance, he said NCC is at bottom valuation and is a buy. He noted both NCC and Jio are good for an investor with a one-year-plus waiting horizon.

recommendations.note: Clear-cut outperformer; buyable at current levels
recommendations.detail: He said Jio looked like a clear-cut outperformer on both 1-week and 3-4-week performance, and can be invested in at current levels for a one-year-plus horizon.

recommendations.note: Do not average a penny stock; large opportunity loss already
recommendations.detail: A caller holding 8,000 Unitech shares bought at Rs 60 asked about averaging. Kutumba Rao agreed one should not average; he noted the stock sat below about five rupees for four-five years and that simply holding so long was already a large opportunity loss, implicitly advising against adding.

recommendations.note: One of his two explicit long-term power picks
recommendations.detail: Asked to name two power stocks in his view, he said his choice would be Adani Power and Tata Power, calling them long-term, low-hype counters. He warned against blindly chasing hot momentum power names (Atlanta Electricals, Quality Power, High Voltage), advising small position sizes and no over-allocation to one sector.

recommendations.note: One of his two explicit long-term power picks
recommendations.detail: Named as his second preferred power pick alongside Adani Power for the long term, as a low-hype counter. He also mentioned Tata Power earlier as a suggestion for a viewer exiting Tech Mahindra to rotate into energy.

recommendations.note: Long-term energy suggestion; buy quality power names on dips
recommendations.detail: For a viewer exiting Tech Mahindra to buy energy stocks, he suggested NTPC for the long term, along with quality transmission/power companies to be bought on dips, and noted nuclear-energy stocks can also be bought.

recommendations.note: Bank stocks stable but entry timing is key
recommendations.detail: Asked about Canara Bank and Siemens Energy, he said both can be added on declines. PSU banks that corrected were recovering; bank stocks are stable but the time of entry is key, and he would accumulate Canara closer to 130 or below 130.
Asked alongside Canara Bank, he said both can be added on declines.

recommendations.note: Split a Rs 5 lakh long-term investment equally with Mazagon Dock
recommendations.detail: A viewer asked which of Garden Reach or Mazagon Dock to invest Rs 5 lakh in for the long term. Kutumba Rao said comparing two same-sector PSUs is the wrong framing and advised putting the amount equally in both.

recommendations.note: Split a Rs 5 lakh long-term investment equally with Garden Reach
recommendations.detail: Paired with Garden Reach in the same viewer question; he advised splitting the Rs 5 lakh equally between the two PSU shipbuilders for the long term rather than choosing one.
